root_cause.text: The `Cookie.maxAge` property was previously changed from `int` to `long`, which broke binary compatibility with existing applications relying on the older API.
root_cause.evidence: The commit message states "Change type of Cookie.maxAge back to int to maintain binary compatibility", and the patch replaces `long` return types and parameters with `int` across multiple cookie-related classes.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `Cookie.maxAge` property was previously changed from `int` to `long`, which broke binary compatibility with existing applications relying on the older API.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The commit message states "Change type of Cookie.maxAge back to int to maintain binary compatibility", and the patch replaces `long` return types and parameters with `int` across multiple cookie-related classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix deprecated Javadoc link to point to the correct org.jboss.netty Cookie class.
intent_record.what: Updated the Javadoc @link reference in the @deprecated tag from an io.netty package to an org.jboss.netty package.
intent_record.how: Modifies documentation only to point developers to the correct replacement class, with no impact on runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor Cookie interface to use int instead of long for maxAge methods.
intent_record.what: Changed the return type of getMaxAge() and maxAge() from long to int, updating the Javadoc to reflect Integer#MIN_VALUE.
intent_record.how: Modifies the interface signature so that maximum age values are represented as integers rather than longs.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change setMaxAge parameter type from long to int.
intent_record.what: Changed the parameter type of the setMaxAge method from long to int.
intent_record.how: Restricts the maxAge parameter to a 32-bit integer, aligning the interface signature with expected standard cookie constraints.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change maxAge variable type from long to int.
intent_record.what: Changed the data type of the maxAge variable from long to int.
intent_record.how: Aligns the variable's type and default minimum value with standard integer bounds.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add explicit int cast to maxAge calculation to ensure correct type assignment.
intent_record.what: Added an explicit (int) cast to the maxAgeMillis calculation.
intent_record.how: Ensures the result of the calculation is properly typed as an integer before assignment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix method signature by changing getMaxAge return type to int.
intent_record.what: Changed the return type of the deprecated getMaxAge method from long to int.
intent_record.how: Updates the method signature to return an integer instead of a long, altering how callers interact with the method.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change maxAge type from long to int to align with standard value ranges.
intent_record.what: Changed the data type of maxAge from long to int and updated its default minimum value accordingly.
intent_record.how: Aligns the variable's capacity and default value with standard integer bounds, reducing memory usage slightly and enforcing appropriate value ranges.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change maxAge variables and return types from long to int to ensure correct type compatibility.
intent_record.what: Changed the return type and parameters of mergeMaxAgeAndExpire from long to int, updating MIN_VALUE constants and casting the result accordingly.
intent_record.how: Modifies the calculation of cookie expiration to use standard 32-bit integers instead of 64-bit longs, ensuring compatibility with standard HTTP cookie APIs that expect max-age as an int.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix max-age parsing to use Integer instead of Long to align with the expected data type.
intent_record.what: Changed the parsing of maxAge from Long to Integer.
intent_record.how: Modifies the cookie parsing logic to parse and bound the max-age value as a 32-bit integer instead of a 64-bit long.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change cookie max age data type from long to int.
intent_record.what: Changed the data type for the cookie's maximum age from long to int in both the maxAge() and setMaxAge() methods, and updated the corresponding Javadoc comments.
intent_record.how: Modifies the API to use a 32-bit integer instead of a 64-bit long for representing the maximum age of a cookie, affecting how callers interact with these methods.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change maxAge field type from long to int.
intent_record.what: Changed the data type of the maxAge variable from long to int and updated its initialization constant.
intent_record.how: Modifies the memory footprint and value bounds of the maxAge field to match standard integer specifications.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change maxAge data type from long to int
intent_record.what: Changed the return type and parameter type of the maxAge methods from long to int.
intent_record.how: Updates the API signature to enforce integer bounds for the cookie's maximum age value.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix incorrect minimum value comparison for cookie max age
intent_record.what: Changed the comparison constant for cookie.maxAge() from Long.MIN_VALUE to Integer.MIN_VALUE.
intent_record.how: Corrects the logic to properly evaluate if a cookie's max age is unset or negative, ensuring the Expires and Max-Age attributes are accurately processed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix the expected max age assertion in CookieDecoderTest to use Integer.MIN_VALUE.
intent_record.what: Changed the expected max age value in the test assertion from Long.MIN_VALUE to Integer.MIN_VALUE.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to correctly assert against a 32-bit integer minimum value instead of a 64-bit long minimum value for the cookie's max age.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix assertion to use Integer.MIN_VALUE instead of Long.MIN_VALUE for max age.
intent_record.what: Changed Long.MIN_VALUE to Integer.MIN_VALUE in the assertion for max age.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to expect an integer minimum value instead of a long.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix test assertion to use Integer.MIN_VALUE for max age.
intent_record.what: Changed the expected value in the assertEquals call for c.getMaxAge() from Long.MIN_VALUE to Integer.MIN_VALUE.
intent_record.how: Updates the test assertion to correctly expect an integer boundary value instead of a long boundary value.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix test assertion to use Integer.MIN_VALUE for max age.
intent_record.what: Changed Long.MIN_VALUE to Integer.MIN_VALUE in an assertion.
intent_record.how: Ensures the test assertion correctly compares against the proper minimum integer value for the cookie's max age.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix integer overflow in cookie max age test by reducing the expiration year and casting the expected value to int.
intent_record.what: Changes the test date from year 9999 to 2080 and explicitly casts the calculated expectedMaxAge from a long to an int.
intent_record.how: Prevents integer overflow issues during the max age calculation by using a closer future date and ensures type compatibility with the underlying Cookie implementation which likely changed maxAge from long to int.

grouping_result.intent: Fix deprecated Javadoc link to point to the correct org.jboss.netty Cookie class.
Change maxAge data type from long to int
Fix integer overflow in cookie max age test by reducing the expiration year and casting the expected value to int.
Fix the expected max age assertion in CookieDecoderTest to use Integer.MIN_VALUE.

consistency_review.core_intent: Change the maxAge data type from long to int across various cookie-related classes and interfaces.
Fix cookie max-age by changing its data type from long to int across the codebase and adjusting tests accordingly.
Fix the minimum value handling and related assertions for cookie max age.

relevance_review.reason: The group only fixes a deprecated Javadoc link, which is a documentation change and unrelated to the binary compatibility issue involving the Cookie.maxAge property.
The group's changes revert the maxAge data type from long back to int, directly addressing the binary compatibility issue described in the vulnerability root cause.
The group directly reverts the data type of the Cookie.maxAge property from long back to int, addressing the binary compatibility issue mentioned in the root cause.

The changes update minimum value handling and assertions for cookie max age to use Integer.MIN_VALUE, which directly correlates with changing the maxAge type back from long to int to restore binary compatibility.

candidates.rationale: Defines maxAge() return type as long, breaking binary compatibility with previous int return type.
Defines setMaxAge() parameter as long, breaking binary compatibility.
Defines maxAge() return type as long in the new cookie package, reflecting the breaking change.
Defines setMaxAge() parameter as long in the new cookie package.
The internal state was changed to long to match the breaking API changes.

vuln_statements.explanation: This defines the maxAge() return type as long, which broke binary compatibility with the previous int return type.
This defines the setMaxAge() parameter as long, contributing to the binary compatibility break.
This defines maxAge() return type as long in the new cookie package, reflecting the breaking change.
This defines setMaxAge() parameter as long in the new cookie package.
The internal state was changed to long to match the breaking API changes.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Validate cookie name and value characters

Motivation:

RFC6265 specifies which characters are allowed in a cookie name
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 402edce586c04d9fdacc82525b2d1a7131a067c8
Author: Stephane Landelle <slandelle@gatling.io>
Date:   Thu May 7 11:09:28 2015 +0200

    Validate cookie name and value characters
    
    Motivation:
    
    RFC6265 specifies which characters are allowed in a cookie name and value.
    
    Netty is currently too lax, which can used for HttpOnly escaping.
    
    Modification:
    
    Backport new RFC6265 compliant Cookie parsers in cookie subpackage.
    Deprecate old Cookie encoders and
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit changes the return type of `getMaxAge()` and the parameter type of `setMaxAge()` from `int` to `long`, which is the exact cause of the binary compatibility breakage described in the vulnerability report.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 997dc9081a5657b28038314eec4e3c363925b90e
Author: Trustin Lee <trustin@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 14:49:26 2009 +0000

    Javadoc

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/jboss/netty/handler/codec/http/Cookie.java b/src/main/java/org/jboss/netty/handler/codec/http/Cookie.java
index da516f37cf..e714e9164a 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/jboss/netty/handler/codec/http/Cookie.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/jboss/netty/handler/codec/http/Cookie.java
@@ -24,32 +24,125 @@ package org.jboss.netty.handler
bic_trace_step.reason: The code defines `getMaxAge()` and `setMaxAge(int maxAge)` using the `int` type. The vulnerability occurs when the type is changed to `long`, which is not present in this commit. This commit only adds Javadoc comments.
